WRONG.question: What is the highest peak in Bangladesh?
WRONG.model_response:
The highest peak in Bangladesh is **Saka Haphong**, which has an elevation of approximately 1,052 meters (3,451 feet). It is located in the Chittagong Hill Tracts near the border with Myanmar. 

*(Note: Historically, Keokradong and Tajingdong were often cited as the highest, but modern geographical surveys have identified Saka Haphong as the actual highest point.)*
